zh.St Mary The Virgin's Church, Sandwich St: 英國肯特桑德維其桑德維其 聖 彼得聖 瑪麗 馮 維吉恩斯 邱奇
zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Ann Attwaters於1439年出生在英國肯特倫哈姆時，她的父親Robert Leigh47歲，母親Petronilla Pimhill41歲。她和Sir John Attwaters Wolvedon至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1558年12月9日在英國肯特桑德維其桑德維其 聖 彼得聖 瑪麗 馮 維吉恩斯 邱奇享年119歲。

nameMeanings.name: Attwaters
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Middle English at (the) water ‘(dweller) beside the water’ (Old English wæter), usually a reference to a pond, stream, or river. Compare Bywater and Waterer .

variants.name: Bywater
meanings: English: topographic name for someone living by a lake or river, from Middle English by ‘by, beside’ + water ‘water’.

Irish: pseudo-translation (due to confusion with sruth ‘stream’) of Gaelic Ó Srutháin ‘descendant of Sruithán’, a personal name from a diminutive of sruith ‘sage, elder’. Bywater is found as the English form of this Gaelic name in County Cork, while in Mayo the usual Anglicization is Ryan .

variants.name: Waterer
meanings: English: locative name from Middle English * waterer, denoting one who lived by a stretch of water such as a village pond or a stream, as proven by the 1547–50 alias Atwater. Topographic names in -er were especially common in Surrey, Sussex, and Hants. This example became particularly numerous as a hereditary surname in Surrey, where it was still most frequent in the 1881 Census. There is no evidence to support Reaney’s view that the name was occupational, from Middle English waterer ‘a person responsible for watering animals’ (attested once before 1500 in MED ), or Cottle’s view that it meant ‘water-seller’.

variants.name: Atwater
meanings: English: topographic name for someone whose dwelling was by a river or lake, Middle English atte water ‘at the water’.
History: This surname was established from an early date in New England. David Atwater was one of the group of settlers who founded the New Haven colony in 1638.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Ann Attwaters was born in 1439, in Lenham, Kent, England, her father, Robert Leigh, was 47 and her mother, Petronilla Pimhill, was 41. She had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Sir John Attwaters Wolvedon. She died on 9 December 1558, in St Mary The Virgin's Church, Sandwich St Peter, Sandwich, Kent, England, at the age of 119.

ko.St Mary The Virgin's Church, Sandwich St: 영국 켄트 샌드위치 샌드위치 스트 피터 스트 메리 테 버긴'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Mary Ann Attwaters(이)가 1439년 영국 켄트 렌함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Leigh(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Petronilla Pimhill(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 Sir John Attwaters Wolvedon(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1558년 12월 9일 영국 켄트 샌드위치 샌드위치 스트 피터 스트 메리 테 버긴'스 c후르치에서 119세의 나이로 사망했다.
